title: Accommodation import â€” scraper resilience (retry, fallback chain, async, provider mitigation, manual path)

tasks.title: Add RETRYABLE_FAILURE_CODES constant and withRetry wrapper to apify-client.ts
tasks.description:
Implement the R1 retry layer closest to the failure point â€” inside `apify-client.ts` so the orchestrator and response contract are unchanged.

**What to create/modify:**
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/adapters/apify-client.ts`
  - Export `RETRYABLE_FAILURE_CODES: ReadonlySet<ImportFailureCode>` = `new Set(['source_blocked', 'timeout'])`
  - Export `WithRetryInput<T>` interface: `{ fn: () => Promise<RunApifyActorResult>; maxRetries?: number; baseDelayMs?: number }`
  - Export `withRetry(input: WithRetryInput): Promise<RunApifyActorResult>` â€” wraps one `runApifyActor` call: if `failureCode` is in `RETRYABLE_FAILURE_CODES`, sleep a jittered delay and retry; otherwise return immediately. Default `maxRetries = 2`, `baseDelayMs = 500`. Jitter: `delay = baseDelayMs + Math.random() * baseDelayMs` (uniform 0â€“100% jitter on the base). Hard cap: never exceed `maxRetries` attempts total (1 original + 2 retries = 3 calls max).
  - Sleep helper: `function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void>` using `new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms))`.
  - JSDoc on all exports.

**Key constraints:**
- `withRetry` does NOT import anything not already in this file.
- Non-retryable codes (`invalid_url`, `credentials_missing`, `provider_error`, `nothing_found`) return immediately without any sleep.
- The function never throws.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `RETRYABLE_FAILURE_CODES` is exported and contains exactly `source_blocked` and `timeout`.
- `withRetry` retries at most `maxRetries` times for retryable codes.
- `withRetry` returns immediately (no sleep) for non-retryable codes.
- `withRetry` returns the first successful result (items.length > 0 and no failureCode).
- If all retries fail, returns the last failure result.

**Testing:** See T-006.

subtasks.title: Export RETRYABLE_FAILURE_CODES constant
Implement sleep helper
Implement withRetry with jittered backoff
Add JSDoc to all new exports

tasks.title: Wire withRetry into AirbnbAdapter.extract()
tasks.description:
Replace the direct `runApifyActor(...)` call in `AirbnbAdapter.extract()` with `withRetry({ fn: () => runApifyActor(...) })` so the Airbnb path automatically retries on `source_blocked`/`timeout`.

**What to modify:**
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/adapters/airbnb.adapter.ts`
  - Import `withRetry` from `./apify-client.js`.
  - Locate the single `runApifyActor(...)` call and wrap it: `const { items, failureCode } = await withRetry({ fn: () => runApifyActor({ token, actor, actorInput, timeoutMs }) })`.
  - No other changes to logic or interfaces.

**Key constraints:**
- The `extract()` method signature and return type (`Promise<RawExtraction>`) are unchanged.
- The `ImportContext` shape is unchanged.
- If `ctx.credentials.apifyToken` or `ctx.credentials.apifyAirbnbActor` is absent, the credential-missing early-return fires BEFORE `withRetry` is called (existing guard).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Airbnb adapter now retries up to 2 times on `source_blocked` or `timeout` before returning a failure.
- All existing airbnb adapter tests still pass.
- The one-line diff is visible: only the `runApifyActor` call site changes.

**Testing:** Covered by T-007 (adapter retry integration tests) plus the existing airbnb adapter test suite must remain green.

subtasks.title: Import withRetry from apify-client
Wrap runApifyActor call site in withRetry
Verify existing adapter tests still pass

tasks.title: Wire withRetry into BookingAdapter.extract()
tasks.description:
Replace the direct `runApifyActor(...)` call in `BookingAdapter.extract()` with `withRetry({ fn: () => runApifyActor(...) })` â€” same pattern as T-002 for the Booking adapter.

**What to modify:**

  - Locate the `runApifyActor(...)` call(s) and wrap with `withRetry`.

**Key constraints:**
- Booking adapter has a JSON-LDâ†’actor escalation path (if JSON-LD succeeds, actor is skipped). Only the actor call site needs the retry wrapper â€” do NOT wrap the JSON-LD fetch.
- The `extract()` method signature and return type are unchanged.

**Acceptance criteria:**

- JSON-LD extraction path is unaffected.
- All existing booking adapter tests still pass.

tasks.title: Implement runFallbackGenericExtract helper function
tasks.description:
Add a private helper `_runFallbackGenericExtract(url: URL, context: ImportContext): Promise<RawExtraction | null>` (or a standalone module-private function) that invokes `GenericAdapter.extract()` against a given URL and returns the result, or `null` on any throw.

**What to create/modify:**
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/accommodation-import.service.ts`
  - Add private method `_runFallbackGenericExtract(url: URL, context: ImportContext): Promise<RawExtraction | null>` to `AccommodationImportService`.
  - The method: instantiates (or reuses) `GenericAdapter`, calls `adapter.extract(url, context)` in a try/catch, returns the result on success or `null` on any throw.
  - This is a single cheap `safeExternalFetch` (the GenericAdapter path) â€” no actor call, no Apify token required.
  - JSDoc explaining: fallback purpose, source platforms it applies to, cost contract (single fetch).

**Key constraints:**
- `GenericAdapter` is already in the adapter registry â€” reuse the existing instance at `this.adapters[this.adapters.length - 1]` (it is always the GenericAdapter) rather than constructing a new one.
- The method never throws â€” returns `null` on any failure.
- No changes to any public interface or the adapter registry.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `_runFallbackGenericExtract` exists as a private method on `AccommodationImportService`.
- It returns a `RawExtraction` when GenericAdapter succeeds.
- It returns `null` when GenericAdapter throws.
- It makes exactly one `safeExternalFetch` call (via the GenericAdapter) â€” no additional network calls.

**Testing:** Covered by T-008 (fallback chain tests).

subtasks.title: Add _runFallbackGenericExtract private method
Reuse existing GenericAdapter instance from adapter registry
Guard with try/catch returning null on throw

tasks.title: Wire R2 fallback chain into AccommodationImportService.importFromUrl()
tasks.description:
After Step 3 (primary extraction), check if the primary adapter returned `source_blocked` AND the source is Airbnb or Booking. If so, call `_runFallbackGenericExtract()` and merge its result as a partial draft.

**What to modify:**
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/accommodation-import.service.ts`
  - Between Step 3 (adapter extraction) and Step 4 (mapRawToDraft), insert R2 fallback logic:
    1. Condition: `raw.failureCode === 'source_blocked' && (source === 'airbnb' || source === 'booking')`
    2. When true: call `await this._runFallbackGenericExtract(effectiveParsedUrl, input.context)`
    3. If fallback returns a non-null `RawExtraction` with at least one useful field (name, summary, or imageUrls): replace `raw` with the fallback extraction but:
       - Force `raw.failureCode = undefined` (fallback succeeded at transport level)
       - Force `partial: true` via the existing `partial` computation path (fallback rarely has name+summary+type)
       - Keep `sourcePlatform` as the original source (`airbnb`/`booking`) for accurate labelling â€” do NOT relabel as `generic`
    4. If fallback returns null or yields zero useful fields: keep original `raw` (the `source_blocked` result); the response degrades normally.
  - Add an inline comment explaining the R2 decision: which sources, cost guard, partial=true invariant.

**Key constraints:**
- Fallback is only attempted ONCE (no retry on the fallback itself).
- `partial: true` on a fallback result â€” the existing `hasMandatoryFields` check handles this automatically since OG/JSON-LD rarely returns all three mandatory fields (name + summary + type).
- Never relabel the `source` field on the response â€” it stays `airbnb`/`booking` so the host knows where the data came from.
- The orchestrator contract (`importFromUrl` signature, `AccommodationImportResponse` shape) is unchanged.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- When Airbnb/Booking primary returns `source_blocked`, the orchestrator attempts GenericAdapter fallback.
- A fallback result with at least name or description yields `partial: true` with real per-field source/confidence in the draft.
- When fallback returns nothing, the response is the original `source_blocked` failure.
- No other source (Google, MercadoLibre, Generic) triggers the fallback.

**Testing:** Covered by T-008.

subtasks.title: Add fallback trigger condition (source_blocked + airbnb/booking)
Call _runFallbackGenericExtract and inspect result
Merge fallback RawExtraction preserving sourcePlatform
Add inline comment explaining R2 decision

tasks.title: Write unit tests for withRetry (retry policy, fail-fast, jitter, cap)
tasks.description:
Comprehensive unit tests for the `withRetry` function in `apify-client.ts`.

**Test file:** `packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation-import/adapters/apify-client.test.ts` (extend existing file â€” add a new `describe('withRetry', ...)` block).

**Test cases to cover (AAA pattern):**
1. Retries exactly `maxRetries` times on `source_blocked` before returning the failure.
2. Retries exactly `maxRetries` times on `timeout` before returning the failure.
3. Does NOT retry on `invalid_url` â€” returns immediately after first call.
4. Does NOT retry on `credentials_missing` â€” returns immediately after first call.
5. Does NOT retry on `provider_error` â€” returns immediately after first call.
6. Does NOT retry on `nothing_found` â€” returns immediately after first call.
7. Returns first success (items.length > 0) without retrying remaining attempts.
8. Defaults to `maxRetries = 2` when not specified.
9. Respects custom `maxRetries` (e.g. 0 = no retry).

**Mocking approach:**
- Mock `sleep` or use `vi.useFakeTimers()` to keep tests fast (no real delays).
- The `fn` parameter is a `vi.fn()` that returns controlled `RunApifyActorResult` values.
- Count how many times `fn` is called to assert retry count.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All 9 test cases pass.
- Tests do not take real wall-clock time (sleep is mocked or fake timers used).
- Total call count assertions are exact (not â‰Ą).

subtasks.title: Set up describe block for withRetry in existing test file
Mock sleep / use vi.useFakeTimers
Write 9 test cases covering retry policy and fail-fast

tasks.title: Write adapter retry integration tests for Airbnb and Booking adapters
tasks.description:
Add integration-level tests to the Airbnb and Booking adapter test files that verify `withRetry` is active and behaves correctly end-to-end through each adapter.

**Test files to extend:**
- `packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation-import/adapters/airbnb.adapter.test.ts`
- `packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation-import/adapters/booking.adapter.test.ts`

**Test cases per adapter (add as a new `describe('R1 retry integration', ...)` block):**
1. When `runApifyActor` returns `source_blocked` twice then succeeds: adapter returns the successful extraction (verifies retry wiring is active).
2. When `runApifyActor` returns `source_blocked` three times (exceeds cap): adapter returns `source_blocked` failure (verifies cap is enforced).
3. When `runApifyActor` returns `credentials_missing` on the first call: adapter returns `credentials_missing` immediately without retrying.

**Mocking:**
- Use `vi.spyOn` or `vi.mock` on `runApifyActor` (or on `withRetry`) to control the sequence of return values.
- Keep tests fast with fake timers or by mocking `sleep` inside `apify-client.ts`.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- 6 tests total (3 per adapter), all passing.
- Tests confirm retry is wired in each adapter without testing `withRetry` itself (that's T-006).

subtasks.title: Add R1 retry integration tests to airbnb.adapter.test.ts
Add R1 retry integration tests to booking.adapter.test.ts
Verify fast execution with mocked sleep/timers

tasks.title: Write service-level tests for R2 fallback chain
tasks.description:
Extend the accommodation import service test file with scenarios covering the R2 fallback chain (Airbnb/Booking primary blocked â†’ GenericAdapter fallback).

**Test file:** `packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation-import/accommodation-import.service.test.ts` (add a new `describe('R2 fallback chain', ...)` block).

**Test cases (AAA pattern):**
1. Airbnb primary returns `source_blocked`; fallback Generic returns name+description via OG: response has `partial: true`, `source: 'airbnb'`, populated `draft.name`, and no `failureCode`.
2. Booking primary returns `source_blocked`; fallback Generic returns name via OG: same assertions for Booking.
3. Airbnb primary returns `source_blocked`; fallback Generic returns nothing (empty extraction): response has `failureCode: 'source_blocked'` (original failure propagated).
4. Airbnb primary returns `credentials_missing` (non-retryable): fallback is NOT triggered; response has `failureCode: 'credentials_missing'`.
5. GooglePlaces primary returns `source_blocked`: fallback is NOT triggered (only Airbnb/Booking trigger it); response has `failureCode: 'source_blocked'`.

**Mocking:**
- Mock the individual adapters (AirbnbAdapter, BookingAdapter, GenericAdapter) via `vi.spyOn` on `extract()`.
- The service is instantiated normally; adapters are spied on after construction.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- 5 test cases, all passing.
- Tests confirm the source-scoping rule (only Airbnb/Booking trigger fallback).
- Tests confirm `partial: true` on a successful fallback.
- Tests confirm fallback failure gracefully propagates original `source_blocked`.

tasks.title: Write ImportFromUrl.client.tsx invariant test â€” form stays usable after full failure
tasks.description:
Add a test file for `ImportFromUrl.client.tsx` asserting R5: after a fully-failed import (all retries and fallback exhausted), the web form is 100% usable for manual entry â€” no spinner trap, submit button re-enabled, non-blaming error note shown.

**Test file to create:** `apps/web/src/components/host/__tests__/ImportFromUrl.test.tsx`

**Test cases (AAA pattern):**
1. After a 200 response with `failureCode: 'source_blocked'`: `isSubmitting` reverts to false (submit button re-enabled), a non-blaming i18n error key is displayed (e.g. `host.importFromUrl.errors.failure.sourceBlocked`), the URL input is still editable.
2. After a 200 response with `failureCode: 'timeout'`: same assertions.
3. After a non-200 HTTP error: submit button re-enabled, network error message shown, URL input is still editable.
4. While the request is in flight (`isSubmitting = true`): submit button is disabled, no error message shown.

**Mocking:**
- Mock `accommodationsImportApi.importFromUrl` via `vi.mock` or `vi.fn()`.
- Use `@testing-library/react` with jsdom environment.
- Mock `createTranslations` to return key-as-value.

**Key assertions:**
- `button[type=button]` (or the submit button) is NOT disabled after failure resolves.
- No `aria-busy` or loading spinner element is present after failure resolves.
- An error message element is visible with the correct i18n key.
- The URL input `value` is preserved (user can edit without re-typing).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- 4 test cases pass.
- No test uses `screen.getByRole('button', { disabled: true })` after the async failure resolves â€” the button must be enabled.

subtasks.title: Create __tests__/ImportFromUrl.test.tsx with jsdom environment header
Mock API and translations
Write 4 test cases covering spinner-clear invariant

tasks.title: Write provider-mitigation runbook (R4)
tasks.description:
Create the R4 runbook documenting how to swap a blocked actor or route through a residential proxy without a code deploy, using the existing `ctx.credentials.apify*Actor` config fields.

**File to create:** `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/docs/provider-mitigation-runbook.md`

**Content to cover:**
1. **Overview:** Why provider mitigation is config-only (actors are already configurable via `ctx.credentials`; no code change needed).
2. **Config fields:** Table listing `apifyAirbnbActor`, `apifyBookingActor`, `apifyGooglePlacesActor` (and any other `apify*Actor` fields present in `ImportContext`), their env var names, current defaults, and which adapters read them.
3. **Runbook â€” swapping an actor:** Step-by-step for an ops person:
   a. Identify the blocked actor and alternative (e.g. `tri_angle/airbnb-rooms-urls-scraper` â†’ `dtrungtin/airbnb-scraper`).
   b. Locate the env var on Coolify (or `hops env-set api HOSPEDA_APIFY_AIRBNB_ACTOR <new-slug>`).
   c. Trigger a redeploy.
   d. Verify with a test import from the admin panel.
4. **Runbook â€” residential proxy:** Note that the Apify actor input accepts a `proxyConfiguration` key; document how to set it if the actor supports it (reference to Apify docs for the specific actor).
5. **Config seam audit result:** State whether all adapter `apify*Actor` fields are correctly sourced from `ImportContext.credentials` (confirming no code seam is missing, or flagging one if found during implementation).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- File exists and covers all 5 content areas.
- No new code is written â€” this is documentation only.
- If a config seam is found missing during the audit (step 5), file a note in this doc AND create a follow-up GitHub issue (do NOT silently skip it).

subtasks.title: Create docs/ directory in accommodation-import service
Write runbook covering actor swap and proxy steps
Document all apify*Actor config fields and their env vars
